title: Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
subtitle: Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to [national estimates](#dod:ilo-national-estimates) and may not be fully comparable across countries.
caption:
Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to national estimates and may not be fully comparable across countries.

This scatter plot compares the share of the working-age population employed in 1980 against the share in 2024 for a selection of countries, with each nation represented by a circle sized according to its total population. The horizontal axis tracks the 2024 employment rate while the vertical axis tracks the 1980 rate, both ranging from 0% to 100%. A diagonal line running from the bottom-left to the top-right would represent no change; the position of the circles relative to this invisible line shows the direction and magnitude of change over the 44-year period. Most labeled countries sit below the diagonal, indicating that female employment rates increased between the two years. The United States, Brazil, and the United Kingdom cluster in the middle of the chart, showing 2024 rates between 50% and 60% compared to 1980 rates around 40% to 50%. Thailand and Burkina Faso appear in the upper-middle section with 1980 rates near 70% to 80% and 2024 rates around 60%. Nigeria and the Bahamas sit far to the right, with 2024 rates exceeding 80% while their 1980 rates were lower, around 35% and 25% respectively. Conversely, Malawi appears high on the vertical axis near 90% for 1980 but sits around 35% on the horizontal axis for 2024. Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Tonga occupy the lower-left quadrant, with rates for both years generally falling between 10% and 30%. The data points show a general pattern where countries with lower employment shares in 1980 tend to have higher shares in 2024, while a few nations show the reverse trend.
